description:
## Problem Statement

Beadsmith renders Issue descriptions and comments as Markdown with one fixed 14px typography system. That compact default matches the developer-tool design, but it is not equally readable for every user or display. Beadsmith has no app-level Settings destination, so the reading preference cannot be changed or remembered.

Markdown typography is a Beadsmith display preference, not Beadwork Issue data and not Workspace data. It must be available even when no Current Workspace is selected, remain the same when the Current Workspace changes, and persist across desktop process restarts.

## Product Outcome

Add a focused app-wide Settings page for Markdown Typography. A Settings button near the bottom of the existing sidebar opens the page in the main content area while the sidebar and Current Workspace control remain visible. The page provides one direct numeric pixel input, immediate preview through the real shared Markdown renderer, automatic persistence, accessible validation and status feedback, and Reset to the 14px default.

A valid value applies to every rendered Issue description and comment. Prose, headings, lists, blockquotes, tables, inline code, and code blocks scale from one base value while preserving their existing visual hierarchy. Non-Markdown application chrome, Issue titles, metadata, navigation, search, and Workspace controls do not scale.

## Resolved Product Decisions

### Scope and ownership

- The preference is app-wide across all Workspaces and desktop sessions.
- It is stored in Beadsmith supported application-data storage, never in Beadwork, a Workspace repository, browser localStorage, or the Workspace Catalog document.
- The initial setting is `markdown.fontSizePx`; the settings shape must leave a coherent place for later prose and monospace font-family fields without implementing those fields now.
- Settings is usable with no Current Workspace and while Workspace loading or recovery UI is present.

### Navigation

- Settings is an app destination in the stable left sidebar, visually separated from Issue List Views and positioned immediately above the Current Workspace selector.
- Opening Settings replaces the main pane; it is not a modal, popover, Issue List View, or new general-purpose routing system.
- While Settings is active, it is the sole sidebar item exposed as the current page. Selecting an Issue List View returns to the Issue Explorer and selects that view.
- Visiting Settings must preserve the active Issue List View, Issue Search query, selected Issue, and list/detail scroll positions when the Current Workspace has not changed.
- If the user changes Current Workspace while Settings is open, the existing confirmed Workspace transition contract remains authoritative: workspace-scoped search, selection, and scroll reset only when the new Workspace commits, while the app-wide Settings destination and typography value remain unchanged.

### Input and validation

- Use one labeled `type=number` input with a visible `px` unit, `min=8`, `max=72`, and `step=1`.
- The default is 14px. Valid values are whole numbers from 8 through 72 inclusive.
- The wider 72px ceiling deliberately preserves the compact default while supporting users who need substantially larger reading text; the old proposed 32px ceiling was too restrictive for an accessibility-oriented preference.
- Do not add a slider, presets, separate prose/code sizes, or fractional values.
- Keep the raw editable draft separate from the last valid applied value. Empty, partial, fractional, non-numeric, and out-of-range drafts show inline validation and do not replace the preview or enqueue persistence.
- Every valid draft immediately becomes the applied session value, updates the preview and all visible Markdown, and is queued for automatic persistence.
- Reset clears any invalid draft, applies 14px immediately, and persists 14px.

### Persistence and failure behavior

- App startup loads Settings once through the typed native interface. Missing state resolves to 14px without warning.
- Malformed, unreadable, or unsupported persisted state resolves to a usable 14px session value plus visible Settings feedback. A subsequent valid edit or Reset may repair the store by writing a valid current document.
- Save operations must preserve latest user intent. If several valid values are entered quickly, an older in-flight completion must not overwrite a newer value in storage or mark a newer value as saved.
- A save failure leaves the newest valid value applied for the current session, keeps the draft intact, and clearly reports that the value is not persisted and may revert after restart. Retry targets the newest valid value, not an older failed request.
- Navigating away from Settings does not cancel an in-flight save because app Settings state belongs to the application shell, not the page lifetime.
- Saving and saved status should be visible but not announced so aggressively that each keystroke becomes noisy for assistive technology; errors must be persistent and accessible.

### Live preview

- The preview uses the same `MarkdownContent` module and typography interface as Issue descriptions and comments; no preview-only Markdown renderer is allowed.
- The sample is short but representative: heading, prose/emphasis/link, list, blockquote, inline code, fenced code, and a table or equivalent compact structured content.
- Preview links retain the same safe HTTP/HTTPS and inert-link behavior as normal Markdown. Preview behavior must not weaken HTML suppression or external-link safety.

## Architecture and Interfaces

- Follow ADR-0001: React owns presentation and Rust owns supported application-data access.
- Follow ADR-0004: new frontend/native behavior uses generated TauRPC types and the established Effect frontend integration style, not raw Tauri invocation.
- Build a deep backend App Settings module: defaulting, persisted-envelope versioning, validation, malformed-state handling, store repair, and save semantics live behind a small load/update interface.
- Keep the persisted schema version internal to the backend module. The frontend receives the user-facing `AppSettings` shape rather than learning storage envelope details.
- Use a dedicated Tauri Store adapter and key/file. Provide an explicit `BEADSMITH_SETTINGS_STORE_PATH` override for desktop acceptance, analogous to but separate from `BEADSMITH_WORKSPACE_STORE_PATH`.
- Build one app-shell settings state model with three distinct concepts: raw draft, newest valid applied value, and last confirmed persisted value. It owns load state, save state, latest-intent write serialization/coalescing, retry, and error presentation data.
- Pass the resolved Markdown base size through app composition to `MarkdownContent`. The renderer remains usable by callers that omit the prop by defaulting to 14px.
- Preserve the current 14px computed appearance by expressing descendant typography and line heights proportionally; avoid accidental nested `em` compounding.
- Do not add Redux, a global event bus, a generic settings framework, or a general router for this one destination.

## User Stories

1. As a user, I can open Settings from the sidebar anywhere in the app.
2. As a user with no Current Workspace, I can still change Markdown typography.
3. As a user, opening Settings keeps the sidebar and Current Workspace control visible.
4. As a user, selecting an Issue List View returns me to the Issue Explorer.
5. As a user, visiting Settings and returning in the same Workspace preserves my view, search, selected Issue, and scroll context.
6. As a user, switching Workspaces while Settings is open follows the normal confirmed-switch reset rules instead of leaking old Issue context.
7. As a user, I enter an exact pixel value directly rather than choosing a slider or preset.
8. As a user, I can choose any whole number from 8 through 72px.
9. As a user, every valid value updates the live preview immediately.
10. As a user, the same valid value updates visible Issue descriptions and comments immediately.
11. As a user, headings, prose, lists, tables, blockquotes, and code remain visually related as size changes.
12. As a user, ordinary app controls and Issue metadata remain at their designed sizes.
13. As a user, invalid or partial text explains the problem without replacing my last readable preview.
14. As a keyboard user, I can reach, edit, reset, retry, and navigate away from Settings predictably.
15. As a screen-reader user, the field, unit, range, help, validation, preview, save state, and errors have explicit relationships.
16. As a user, valid changes save automatically without an extra Save button.
17. As a user typing quickly, the last valid value I entered is the value that survives restart.
18. As a user, a save failure does not discard my current readable size, and I can retry the newest value.
19. As a user with unreadable or unsupported stored settings, the app remains usable at 14px and lets me repair the preference.
20. As a returning user, the saved size is restored after the desktop process restarts.
21. As a multi-Workspace user, the same size follows me between Workspaces.
22. As a user, Reset restores and saves the 14px design default.
23. As a future maintainer, prose and monospace font-family controls can join the same Markdown Typography settings shape without moving persistence or replacing the renderer interface.

## Cross-Cutting Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Settings is available and functional with or without a Current Workspace.
- [ ] One direct numeric input supports whole-number values 8-72px, default 14px; no slider or presets exist.
- [ ] Valid drafts apply immediately; invalid drafts preserve the last valid applied value and never persist.
- [ ] Automatic saves are latest-intent safe, and save failure/retry semantics are visible and deterministic.
- [ ] Reset clears validation, applies 14px, and persists it.
- [ ] Settings navigation preserves same-Workspace Issue Explorer interaction state and respects confirmed Workspace-switch resets.
- [ ] Issue descriptions, comments, and the preview use the same shared Markdown renderer interface.
- [ ] The 14px default is visually unchanged; every supported value scales the complete Markdown hierarchy without clipping or accidental compounding.
- [ ] App chrome and non-Markdown Issue UI do not scale.
- [ ] Missing settings silently default; malformed/read failures visibly fall back and can be repaired; save failures keep the session usable.
- [ ] App settings use dedicated backend-owned Tauri storage, generated TauRPC types, and Effect frontend integration.
- [ ] Real desktop acceptance proves a true process restart with the same isolated settings store, not only a renderer refresh.

## Testing Strategy

- Pure Rust tests at the App Settings module interface cover missing/default state, valid persisted state, all validation boundaries, malformed JSON/value shape, unsupported version, store read failure, store save failure, repair after load failure, and no in-memory persisted commit on failed save.
- TauRPC contract tests cover load/update types, typed failures, default response behavior, and regenerated TypeScript bindings.
- Shared Markdown renderer tests cover the article base size, proportional descendant ratios and line heights, exact 14px compatibility, min/max values, and all existing safe Markdown/GFM/link behavior.
- Frontend tests cover Settings availability without a Workspace, navigation/current-page semantics, same-Workspace context preservation, Workspace-switch reset interaction, loading, direct entry, the invalid draft matrix, live preview, app-wide application, Reset, latest-intent write ordering, save failure/retry, load failure repair, and accessible names/descriptions/status.
- Real desktop WebDriver acceptance uses disposable Beadwork fixtures plus separate isolated Workspace and Settings stores. One process sets and confirms a non-default value; a second process launched against the same stores proves restoration in the input, preview, Issue description, and comment.
- Each implementation slice runs its focused tests plus applicable Rust formatting/tests, generated binding verification, `pnpm test`, TypeScript checking, Ultracite checks, React Doctor for React changes, frontend build, and the focused desktop command where required.

## Out of Scope

- Prose font-family selection.
- Monospace font-family selection.
- Separate prose and code font-size controls.
- Font weight, color, theme, letter spacing, or general UI scaling.
- Per-Workspace settings, Beadwork-backed settings, device sync, import, or export.
- A general router or settings framework.
- Markdown editing, syntax changes, raw HTML support, sanitizer changes, or new link protocols.
- Applying the setting to Issue titles, metadata, navigation, search, or Workspace controls.
- Fractional values or values outside 8-72px.
- Migration from any released settings schema; Beadsmith is pre-release and this is the first App Settings document.

## Delivery Slices

1. `bsm-al6.1` implements the backend App Settings module, dedicated store adapter, typed TauRPC interface, and generated bindings.
2. `bsm-al6.2` makes shared Markdown typography accept one proportional base size while preserving the 14px design.
3. `bsm-al6.3` integrates app-shell settings state, safe automatic persistence, Settings navigation, direct input, preview, and Issue Explorer behavior.
4. `bsm-al6.4` proves the complete path through two real desktop launches and documents the focused command.

The frontend slice intentionally stays one child: navigation, draft/applied/confirmed state, preview, and app-wide renderer composition form one user-visible transaction and would create awkward temporary interfaces if split. Backend persistence and Markdown rendering remain independent parallel foundations after the epic is unblocked.

No open Workspace-management bead is a prerequisite for this feature. Closed `bsm-kia.2` and `bsm-kia.3` already supplied the Workspace persistence/catalog behavior needed by the Settings acceptance path. Closed `bsm-kia.4` and `bsm-kia.7` touch some of the same App, RPC, generated-binding, and E2E files, so an implementer must start from the latest landed main and resolve ordinary rebase conflicts; overlap is coordination risk, not a dependency. Open `bsm-kia.5` is final Workspace proof/documentation, and `bsm-kia.6` is optional renderer-transition cleanup. Neither blocks App Settings. `bsm-al6.1` and `bsm-al6.2` may proceed in parallel.

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[bsm-al6.1 App Settings persistence and TauRPC] --> C[bsm-al6.3 Settings page and app integration]
    B[bsm-al6.2 Configurable Markdown typography] --> C
    C --> D[bsm-al6.4 Two-launch desktop acceptance]
```

## Deviation Policy

Implementation may choose local names and file placement that better match the merged code, but it must preserve the product semantics and interfaces above. Stop and update the bead before changing the valid range, persistence ownership, latest-intent behavior, navigation model, same-Workspace preservation, Workspace-switch reset behavior, accessibility contract, or renderer safety. No new ADR is expected because the plan follows accepted Tauri/TauRPC/store decisions; create one only if implementation reveals a new hard-to-reverse trade-off.

comments.text:
## Planning Audit — 2026-07-15

Re-read `AGENTS.md`, `CONTEXT.md`, accepted ADRs, the design/Issue Detail research, shared Markdown source/tests, App/IssueExplorer state ownership, Workspace persistence/RPC patterns, and the real WebDriver harness before revising this epic.

### Known knowns confirmed from territory

- `MarkdownContent` is the single renderer used for Issue descriptions and comments and already owns HTML/link safety.
- The app shell/sidebar is the correct navigation seam; there is no router and none is needed.
- Issue Search and selected Issue are local to `IssueExplorer`, so replacing it by unmounting would silently violate the promised preservation behavior.
- Tauri Store is already registered and backend-owned; TauRPC is the required typed native interface; Effect is the required frontend integration style.
- Workspace transitions are actively being stabilized and touch the same App/RPC/E2E surfaces.
- The existing WebDriver suite uses renderer refreshes for some startup reads, but a refresh is not proof of persistence across native process restart.

### Highest-risk blindspots found and resolved in the beads

1. **Out-of-order autosaves**: immediate valid edits can launch overlapping writes, allowing an older completion to become durable after newer intent. The revised plan requires a draft/applied/confirmed model plus serialized/coalesced latest-intent writes and deterministic Retry.
2. **False persistence proof**: a renderer refresh leaves the Tauri process alive. The acceptance child now requires two real built-app launches sharing one isolated Settings store.
3. **Accessibility ceiling**: the originally proposed 32px maximum was too low for users who need materially larger reading text. The accepted domain remains bounded but expands to 8-72px.
4. **Settings without a Workspace**: an app-wide preference must not disappear behind the empty Workspace chooser or memory recovery UI. Availability is now explicit.
5. **Navigation ambiguity**: Settings and the remembered Issue List View must not both present as the current page. Current-page semantics and return behavior are now specified.
6. **Workspace changes while Settings is open**: same-Workspace context preservation must not defeat the established rule that confirmed Workspace changes reset workspace-scoped search/selection. The revised plan separates these cases.
7. **Save failure semantics**: reverting the visible value would be jarring, while pretending it saved would be misleading. The plan keeps the newest value active for the session, marks it not persisted, and retries the newest intent.
8. **Schema leakage**: making React carry a storage version would create a shallow interface. The backend owns the versioned envelope; React receives user-facing App Settings.
9. **Relative CSS compounding**: nested code elements can accidentally apply `em` ratios twice, and fixed 24px line height would clip very large text. The renderer child now defines proportional line-height and one-time ratio rules.
10. **Concurrent Workspace work**: settings persistence and app integration overlap the runtime/RPC/App/E2E files still settling under Workspace management. `bsm-kia.5` now blocks the backend settings child; the renderer foundation can proceed independently.

### Decomposition decision

Kept four children rather than creating more. Backend persistence and shared Markdown rendering are independent parallel modules. Frontend navigation, draft/applied/confirmed state, automatic persistence, preview, and app-wide propagation form one coherent user transaction; splitting them would create temporary unused interfaces and duplicate integration work. Real desktop proof remains a separate final gate because it has distinct harness/process-lifecycle concerns.

### Domain/ADR decision

No new ADR or `CONTEXT.md` term is required. App Settings is local presentation state and follows existing accepted architecture rather than introducing a new hard-to-reverse trade-off. If implementation reveals a genuinely new persistence or navigation decision, update this epic before coding around it.

## Dependency Graph Correction — 2026-07-15

Moved the cross-feature dependency from child `bsm-al6.1` to the parent epic: `bsm-kia.5 blocks bsm-al6`.

Reason: Beadwork propagates effective blockers through an epic subtree. A blocker on only the backend child still removed the entire Settings subtree, including the supposedly independent renderer child, from `bw ready`, while making the displayed graph look as though only one child waited. The parent-level dependency now documents the real workflow honestly: finish the overlapping Workspace/App/RPC/E2E stabilization first, then allow persistence and renderer foundations to proceed in parallel.

This is sequencing, not a product dependency. `bsm-al6.2` remains technically independent; it is deliberately delayed to keep the active repository work focused and to ensure all children implement against the same landed app shell.

## Dependency Reassessment — 2026-07-15

Removed `bsm-kia.5 blocks bsm-al6` after checking the actual child scope and code territory. `bsm-kia.5` is a final Workspace WebDriver/documentation consolidation task; it does not create a settings runtime, navigation interface, persistence capability, or renderer contract needed by this epic. `bsm-kia.6` explicitly describes optional cleanup and is also not a prerequisite.

Closed Workspace children already provide the only behavior used by Settings: Workspace Catalog persistence/restoration (`bsm-kia.2`/`.3`) and the current App shell. Closed `.4`/`.7` may still land overlapping edits in `App.tsx`, `rpc.rs`, generated bindings, and E2E harness files, but ordinary branch/rebase conflict risk is not a semantic dependency. The correct graph therefore begins with `bsm-al6.1` and `.2` in parallel.

title: Add app-wide Markdown typography settings
